claim: Context graphs add to AI agents the why behind decisions: policies, rules, and prior decisions, not just knowledge and tools
actionable: When building agent systems, store decision rationale and policies in a graph database alongside facts to enable explainable decisions
source_title: Context Graphs for Explainable, Decision-Aware AI Agents

claim: Reference class validation: before an agent acts on a decision, it must know which reference class it belongs to (e.g., 99% or 1% group)
actionable: Have agents classify themselves into risk categories before decisions: is this drug safe for 99% or only 1% of population?
source_title: Context Graphs for Explainable, Decision-Aware AI Agents

claim: Decision-making framework has five stages: frame problem with causality, pull global rules, run risk-value analysis, act or escalate, record reasoning
actionable: Implement five-stage decision framework in agent systems where agents frame context, check rules, analyze risk, and escalate when uncertain
source_title: Context Graphs for Explainable, Decision-Aware AI Agents

claim: Store every agent decision and its full reasoning chain in a graph as precedent for future agents to reference
actionable: When agents make decisions, log the decision, reasoning, and outcome to graph database as reusable precedent for future similar situations
source_title: Context Graphs for Explainable, Decision-Aware AI Agents

claim: Multi-agent systems require a decision framework where one agent proposes options, another agent with authority decides, third escalates if needed
actionable: In multi-agent systems, separate agents into roles: analyst (proposes), decision-maker (acts or escalates), escalation handler (human oversight)
source_title: Context Graphs for Explainable, Decision-Aware AI Agents
